name: Apache ShardingSphere
description: Distributed SQL middleware for sharding, scalability and security; originated at JD.com, now Apache TLP.

seo.title: Apache ShardingSphere — DBaaS / Serverless Databases
seo.meta_description: Apache ShardingSphere: Distributed SQL middleware for sharding, scalability and security; originated at JD.com, now Apache TLP.

when_to_use: Horizontal MySQL sharding without changing application code
Distributed SQL routing across 100+ database shards
Adding read-write splitting and shadow DB to legacy Java apps
Data encryption and masking at the middleware layer
Zero-downtime migration from single MySQL to sharded cluster

pros: Transparent sharding for existing SQL databases
Works as JDBC driver or proxy
cons: Overlap with native sharding in Vitess
review.verdict: Powerful sharding middleware for MySQL and PostgreSQL, requires real DBA chops to operate confidently in production.
review.best_for: Teams needing transparent sharding layer over existing MySQL or PostgreSQL
review.not_for: Greenfield projects where TiDB or CockroachDB offer simpler distributed SQL
